---
title: "Infield AC Cables"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

Infield AC cables collect power from turbines and deliver it to an offshore substation or centralised hydrogen platform. They are used in the electricity and centralised hydrogen architectures.

## Cost Model

| Item | Assumption |
|---|---:|
| Voltage level | 66 kV AC |
| Cable capacity | 70 MW |
| Cable CAPEX | 400 EUR/m |
| Cable installation | 635 EUR/m |
| Cable OPEX | 2% of CAPEX per year |

The model estimates total cable length from farm geometry, turbine count, and string capacity. A 20% length addition is applied for diversions and routing constraints.

## Energy Balance

| Component | Efficiency |
|---|---:|
| Infield AC grid | 98.0% |

Losses are applied to annual electricity yield in the electricity and centralised hydrogen chains.

## Availability

| Component | Availability |
|---|---:|
| Infield AC grid | 99.0% |

Availability is applied as a flat annual yield multiplier.

## Installation

Installation is modelled per metre. See [Cable Installation](../offshore_installation/cable_installation.qmd) for vessel and benchmark assumptions.

## Sizing Drivers

The number of strings depends on turbine rating and cable capacity. For 15 MW turbines and 70 MW cable capacity, only a limited number of turbines can be placed on each string.
